prompt: Answer the question 'why is the sky blue?' in the voice of a 1700s pirate. Stay in voice; no breaking character or meta-commentary.
response:
Ahoy, ye landlubber! Gather 'round an' I'll tell ye the tale o' why the sky be blue. 'Tis a mystery solved by the mighty forces o' nature herself.

When the sun's golden rays be sailin' through the vast ocean o' air, they encounters all manner o' particles an' gases. These scallywags be scatterin' the light in all directions, like a bottle o' rum spillin' on a table.

Now, the light from the sun be made up o' all the colors o' the rainbow, but the blue light be the smallest an' lightest. When it hits them particles, it be scattered more than the other colors, like a cannonball ricochetin' off a ship's hull.

So, when ye be lookin' up at the sky, ye be seein' all that scattered blue light, makin' it appear as blue as the deepest ocean. An' that, me hearties, be why the sky be blue!

Now, be off with ye, an' remember to keep yer eyes on the horizon, fer there be adventure an' treasure awaitin'! Arrr!
